home.subtitle: Understand the five phases of a cyberattack.

mascot_script.text: Hi! I'm Neko, your guide through the Cyber Kill Chain, nyaa~!
I'll walk you through seven short lessons, then a short quiz. Hit Begin Learning whenever you're ready!

lessons.label: The Framework
lessons.title: Every cyberattack tells the same story.

mascot_script.text: Welcome to lesson 1! The Cyber Kill Chain was developed by Lockheed Martin as a map of how every cyberattack moves forward.
Every breach, big or small, typically needs to complete each of these 5 phases to succeed.
Click any circle below to preview that phase.

intro_bullets: Developed by Lockheed Martin (2011) as a defensive framework.
Describes 5 sequential phases every attack must complete.
Breaking any single phase stops the entire attack.

chain_nodes.label: Recon
chain_nodes.name: Reconnaissance
chain_nodes.desc: The attacker researches the target, scanning networks, harvesting emails, and mapping out the organization's attack surface.

chain_nodes.label: Arm & Deliver
chain_nodes.name: Arm & Deliver
chain_nodes.desc: The attacker crafts a tailored weapon (malware or a malicious document) and delivers it via phishing or compromised channels.

chain_nodes.label: Initial Compromise
chain_nodes.name: Initial Compromise
chain_nodes.desc: The weapon detonates: a vulnerability is triggered, code executes, and the attacker locks in persistent access through an encrypted command channel.

chain_nodes.label: Escalate Privs
chain_nodes.name: Escalate Privileges
chain_nodes.desc: From a regular user foothold, the attacker hunts for plaintext cloud credentials and chains AWS IAM roles to reach account-admin.

chain_nodes.label: Exfiltrate
chain_nodes.name: Exfiltration
chain_nodes.desc: The endgame: sensitive data is located, compressed, and secretly transferred to the attacker's external servers.

mascot_script.text: Phase 1 · Reconnaissance. The attacker has picked ACME Corp as their target.
This is PASSIVE recon — just Google, LinkedIn, job posts, public leaks. The victim never notices a thing.
Hit Start Scan to watch them harvest employee emails from LinkedIn!
Phase 2 · Arm & Deliver. With the target list in hand, they build a weapon and pick a delivery ride.
The #1 ride? Email. Walk through the three steps to craft a malicious doc and phish poor Mike.
Phase 3 · Initial Compromise. Mike opens what LOOKS like an invoice. He sees a warning. Just one careless click…
Click the glowing Enable Content button to fire the macro and watch the reverse shell fly back to the attacker.
Once the foothold is live, open the encrypted C2 tunnel and run commands straight from the attacker's console.
Phase 4 · Escalate Privileges. The shell on Mike's box is just a regular user — useless for the crown jewels.
Modern attackers love this trick: grep the disk for ~/.aws/credentials. Devs leave plaintext keys behind ALL the time.
Walk the three steps: hunt the file, read the keys, then sts:AssumeRole into the org-admin role. Game over.
Phase 5 · Exfiltration. Remember the OrganizationAccountAdmin role you assumed in Phase 4? It can read every S3 bucket, every RDS snapshot, every Secrets Manager entry in the account.
The attacker just runs aws s3 cp and aws secretsmanager get-secret-value with their admin session — the API logs it as a totally normal call.
Click any AWS resource to siphon it into the attacker's S3 bucket, or smash-and-grab everything at once.
Real-world time! This is MGM Resorts, September 2023. Scattered Spider used every phase you just learned, in exact order.
A single 10-minute phone call to the IT help desk caused over $100M in losses. Social engineering is scary powerful!
When you're ready, hit Start the Quiz to prove what you learned!

steps.title: LinkedIn & Social Profiling
steps.desc: Attackers profiled an MGM IT staffer by scraping LinkedIn and other social media — his role, his colleagues, even his speaking style.

steps.title: The 10-Minute Phone Call
steps.desc: They called the IT help desk impersonating the employee and convinced the agent to reset his credentials — no malware needed.

steps.title: Okta Tenant Takeover
steps.desc: With the reset credential, they signed into MGM's Okta tenant, dropped a session cookie, and pivoted into on-prem Active Directory for a durable foothold.

steps.title: Secondary Identity Provider
steps.desc: They added a second, attacker-controlled identity provider to MGM's federation, minting their own admin tokens at will.

steps.title: Ransomware & $100M in Losses
steps.desc: BlackCat ransomware was deployed across thousands of ESXi hosts; slot machines, room keys, and reservations went dark for 10 days.

roster.name: Neko
roster.subtitle: Your Cyber Kill Chain guide
roster.flavor: A friendly cat-girl analyst who narrates every lesson and walks you through the kill chain. Always unlocked.

roster.name: Cyber Inu
roster.subtitle: Blue-team boss · cybernetic SOC sentinel
roster.flavor: A half-machine Shiba-eared analyst who lives wired into the SOC. His cybernetic HUD eye sees every packet on the wire. Defeat him once in the Final Battle and he joins your roster as a selectable narrator.
roster.unlock_hint: Defeat Cyber Inu in the Final Battle.

challenges.label: Final Challenge
challenges.title: Final Battle: Cyber Inu
challenges.desc: One challenge to rule them all. Face Cyber Inu, the cybernetic blue-team boss, in a turn-based duel. Pick the right attack against his current defensive posture and win the day.

mascot_script.text: This is it. The Final Battle pits you against Cyber Inu, a security defender.
Your objective is to clear all five phases in 12 turns: Recon → Arm/Deliver → Initial Compromise → Escalate → Exfil before his Detection bar maxes out.
Cyber Inu will deploy defensive measures to counter your attacks.

ir_move.name: Incident Response
ir_move.tagline: Calls in the IR team. Detection spikes hard.
ir_move.flavor: He calls in the full IR team. Logs replayed, anomalies surfaced — your stealth window is closing fast.

postures.name: Honeypotting
postures.blurb: He's laying decoys to bait scanners. Map the REAL surface and ignore the bait.

postures.name: Hunting
postures.blurb: He's deep in his SIEM hunting odd packets. A human-layer attack will slip past.

postures.name: Hardening
postures.blurb: He's tightening EDR + egress filters. Only a quiet, in-memory foothold slips past.

postures.name: Key Rotation
postures.blurb: He's rotating every IAM credential he can see. You'll need to grab keys he hasn't reached yet.

postures.name: Triaging
postures.blurb: He's chasing IoCs in his dashboard. Move the data while his eyes are on the wrong screen.

phases.label: Reconnaissance
phases.intro_line: Cyber Inu hasn't pinged you yet — his HUD eye is still calibrating. Map his real attack surface before he raises an alarm.

player_moves.name: LinkedIn Harvest
player_moves.tagline: Scrape real employees. Reveals his next posture.
player_moves.flavor: You build a target list of real employees from LinkedIn.
player_moves.strong_flavor: Critical insight! While he plants decoys, you focus on real names and pin his next move.

player_moves.name: Subdomain Enum
player_moves.tagline: Brute-force DNS. Solid intel, no posture bonus.
player_moves.flavor: DNS brute-force surfaces three forgotten subdomains.

player_moves.name: Job-Board Mining
player_moves.tagline: Read job postings. Easy to fool with fake roles.
player_moves.flavor: Glassdoor and job posts fill in the org chart.
player_moves.weak_flavor: He seeded fake postings — you waste the turn chasing ghost roles.

opponent_move.name: Decoy Directory
opponent_move.flavor: He spots unfamiliar LDAP queries hitting his decoy directory and tags your IP.

phases.label: Arm & Deliver
phases.intro_line: Target list is loaded. Pick a delivery channel and arm the payload.

player_moves.name: Spear-Phish Email
player_moves.flavor: A perfectly-crafted invoice email lands in his inbox.
player_moves.strong_flavor: Critical insight! He's deep in SIEM packet logs — the human-layer email walks right past his eyes.

player_moves.name: Watering Hole
player_moves.flavor: His favorite security blog now serves a malicious script.

player_moves.name: Zero-Day Exploit
player_moves.tagline: Detonate a CVE on his edge. 20% crit: skip straight to P4.
player_moves.flavor: An unpublished CVE detonates against ACME's perimeter and drops a session.
player_moves.crit_jump_flavor: Zero-day chained! The exploit dropped a session AND lifted IAM creds out of the user-data — Compromise skipped, half of Escalate already done.

opponent_move.name: Inbound Threat Hunt
opponent_move.flavor: He sweeps his SIEM for new domains and weird email patterns — your delivery channel just got a louder signature.

phases.label: Initial Compromise
phases.intro_line: Payload landed. Time to gain a foothold AND lock it in before his EDR clamps down.

player_moves.name: Macro Trigger
player_moves.tagline: Tricks user into Enable Content. Quiet in-memory loader.
player_moves.flavor: He clicks 'Enable Content' and the macro fires a reflective loader.
player_moves.strong_flavor: Critical insight! While he hardens the perimeter, the user-layer macro walks straight past his EDR.

player_moves.name: Stolen VPN Creds
player_moves.tagline: Sign in with credentials from a 3rd-party breach.
player_moves.flavor: A credential pair from last quarter's HaveIBeenPwned dump signs straight into ACME's VPN.

player_moves.name: Persistence Backdoor
player_moves.tagline: Schedule a recurring task. Loud on EDR.
player_moves.flavor: A scheduled task ensures access survives reboots.
player_moves.weak_flavor: His freshly tuned EDR sees the new schtasks call instantly and tags the host.

opponent_move.name: EDR Quarantine
opponent_move.flavor: His EDR catches part of the exploit chain mid-detonation. The SOC notices.

phases.label: Escalate Privileges
phases.intro_line: You're a regular user on Mike's box. Find admin creds before Cyber Inu rotates them out from under you.

player_moves.name: AWS Cred Hunt
player_moves.flavor: You grep the home directory and find an AKIA access key sitting in plaintext.
player_moves.strong_flavor: Critical insight! You snatch the key seconds before his rotation policy kills it.

player_moves.name: IMDSv2 Steal
player_moves.flavor: From the compromised EC2 host you grab short-lived STS creds off the metadata service.

player_moves.name: AssumeRole Pivot
player_moves.flavor: You call sts:AssumeRole on the OrganizationAccountAdmin role and assume admin.
player_moves.weak_flavor: His rotation already invalidated the upstream key — the AssumeRole call returns AccessDenied.

opponent_move.name: IAM Credential Rotation
opponent_move.flavor: He cycles long-lived access keys and pipes CloudTrail straight into his SIEM. Your AssumeRole noise lights up the dashboard.

phases.label: Exfiltration
phases.intro_line: You're inside. The crown jewels are reachable. Move them out before forensics catches up.

player_moves.name: Data Exfiltration
player_moves.flavor: Customer DBs, source code, and crypto keys stream toward your C2.
player_moves.strong_flavor: Critical insight! He's still triaging the breach while the crown jewels are already on the wire.

player_moves.name: Ransomware Detonate
player_moves.tagline: Lock & ransom. As effective as exfil — different objective.
player_moves.flavor: BlackCat encrypts thousands of ESXi hosts.
player_moves.strong_flavor: Critical insight! He's still triaging the breach while BlackCat finishes encrypting the cluster.

player_moves.name: Wipe Tracks
player_moves.tagline: Clear logs. Hides the exfil but he sees you do it.
player_moves.flavor: Event logs cleared. Forensics window collapsing.
player_moves.weak_flavor: He's reading those exact logs in real time and watches them vanish line by line.

opponent_move.name: DLP Triage Sweep
opponent_move.flavor: His DLP profile flags the throughput spike and starts inspecting outbound traffic. The clock is ticking.

exfil.title: Crown Jewels Stolen
exfil.text: Customer databases, source code, and signing keys reached your C2 before Cyber Inu's IR team closed the window. Mission complete — Cyber Inu joins your roster.

ransom.title: Network Held Hostage
ransom.text: BlackCat encryption rolled across thousands of ESXi hosts before the SOC could pull the plug. Mission complete — Cyber Inu joins your roster.

wipe.title: Ghost Out
wipe.text: Logs scrubbed, breadcrumbs erased — but a real campaign needs an objective. Mission complete with caveats — Cyber Inu joins your roster.

detection.title: Incident Response Activated
detection.text: Cyber Inu's SOC pieced your campaign together before you could exfil. The blue team holds the line. Try the battle again from the Quiz tab.

timeout.title: Engagement Window Expired
timeout.text: You burned too many turns inside Cyber Inu's network. The SOC's threat-hunt loop caught up with you. Try the battle again from the Quiz tab.
